taxonomy.statement: A thalion is a cell in the Thalean graph.

identity.primary_interpretation: symmetric transport cavity with a binary internal cover degree of freedom

provenance.construction_summary: Originally derived from ordered Petrie decagon adjacency in the dodecahedron, then elevated to a canonical graph object with independent symmetry, spectral, distance, antipodal, and local-state-space structure.

antipodal_structure.interpretation: Each thalion has a unique antipodal thalion at distance 6, and the graph quotients to a 30-vertex 4-regular graph under antipodal pairing.

distance_regular_prefix.interpretation: The graph is locally distance-regular through radius 3.

distance_structure.interpretation: The graph is not fully distance-regular; far-shell structure splits beginning at radius 4.

spectral_structure.interpretation: The spectrum contains golden-ratio-derived values consistent with dodecahedral/icosahedral symmetry.

parity_split.description: The signed 2-cover decomposes exactly into symmetric and antisymmetric sectors under sheet exchange.

distinguished_swap_mode.interpretation: Uniform sheet-flip direction in the doubled local frame.

local_state_space.interpretation: In the natural local doubled frame, the distinguished swap mode leaves a 7-dimensional complementary transport space.

dynamics.interpretation: The graph behaves as a finite resonant transport cavity.

petrie_spacing.interpretation: Shared edge-fiber positions in ordered decagon cycles are separated by half-decagon spacing 5.

b_series_shadow.interpretation: Compressed mnemonic of Thalean transport: local regularity horizon, Petrie half-decagon spacing, swap-complement local transport space, doubled local saturation, reclosure, parity seal.

generator_series.interpretation: Structural generator ladder: seed, polarity, closure, pentagonal generator, doubled Petrie closure.

interpretive_notes.primary_note: The Thalean graph is the full object; a thalion is one cell in that graph.
interpretive_notes.qm_translation: A finite 60-site tight-binding graph Hamiltonian with a built-in Z2 internal degree of freedom from a signed double cover.
interpretive_notes.geometric_note: The Petrie construction is the provenance channel, not the full identity of the object.
